task_completion.description: A single track is created and named 'Oral History Archive - Tape 042'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour editor window, there should be a track header on the left side with the name 'Oral History Archive - Tape 042' (or containing keywords like 'Tape 042', 'Oral History').

task_completion.description: The Side A audio file is placed at the very beginning of the track.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An audio region (representing Side A) is visible on the 'Oral History Archive - Tape 042' track, with its left edge aligned exactly at the 0:00:00 mark on the timeline.

task_completion.description: The Side B audio file is placed on the same track with a 2-second overlap over the end of Side A.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second audio region (Side B) is placed on the same track. Since Side A is 15 seconds long, Side B should start at the 13-second mark on the timeline, visibly overlapping the end of Side A.

task_completion.description: A session marker named 'Tape Flip' is added exactly at the start of the Side B segment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the markers ruler/lane above the timeline, there is a marker labeled 'Tape Flip'. The vertical line of this marker aligns perfectly with the start of the Side B audio region (around the 13-second mark).

task_completion.description: The session is exported as a WAV file to the specified archival exports directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Ardour 'Export' dialog or a file manager/terminal window showing the successful creation of 'master_tape_042.wav' in the '/home/ga/Audio/archival_exports/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to arrange the audio and create markers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Ardour application open with the audio regions being manipulated on the timeline, rather than the agent just using terminal commands or text editors to modify session files.

integrity.description: The agent used Ardour's export functionality to create the WAV file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ardour 'Export' dialog is visible in the screenshots, rather than the agent using a terminal command like 'cp' or 'ffmpeg' to bypass the DAW.

integrity.description: The agent imported the actual audio files from the specified directory rather than creating dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The audio regions on the timeline show actual waveforms, and the import dialog or file browser shows the files being taken from '/home/ga/Audio/digitization/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The audio sample rate is 44.1kHz. Side A is 15 seconds long. To achieve a 2-second overlap, Side B and the 'Tape Flip' marker must be positioned at exactly 13.0 seconds (573,300 samples) on the timeline, with a tolerance of +/- 0.5 seconds (22,050 samples). The track name must contain keywords like 'Tape 042' or 'Oral History Archive'. The final export must be a WAV file located in /home/ga/Audio/archival_exports/.
